content.text:
[
  {
    "title": "Frequently Asked Questions about Nimiq PoS Migration",
    "path": "/archive/faqs",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/archive/faqs"
  },
  {
    "title": "Legacy PoW → PoS Migration",
    "path": "/archive",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/archive"
  },
  {
    "title": "Nimiq PoW (Legacy)",
    "path": "/archive/nimiq-pow",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/archive/nimiq-pow"
  },
  {
    "title": "PoS Migration Guide for Node Operators",
    "path": "/archive/node-operators",
    "description": "Our migration guides are designed for users who want to actively participate in the transition process. This specific guide is for those who want to run the migration process without becoming validators (if you want to become one of the first validators from the start of the PoS chain, please refer to this guide). It is suitable for:",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/archive/node-operators"
  },
  {
    "title": "Activation Guide",
    "path": "/archive/validator-activation",
    "description": "This guide is part of the Nimiq PoW to PoS migration process and is intended for users who have already registered as validators during the Validator Registration Phase by October 6th, 2024. If you missed the registration deadline, you can still participate in the activation as an observer by following this guide.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/archive/validator-activation"
  },
  {
    "title": "Registration Guide",
    "path": "/archive/validator-registration",
    "description": "This document guides users through the registration process to become one of the first validators on the Nimiq PoS chain. It is part of the overall documentation for the transition from PoW to PoS. For more technical details on the migration, see Migration Technical Details.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/archive/validator-registration"
  },
  {
    "title": "Design Kit",
    "path": "/design-kit",
    "description": "Download Nimiq brand assets and design resources.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/design-kit"
  },
  {
    "title": "API Reference",
    "path": "/hub/api-reference",
    "description": "Summary of Hub API methods, request types, and access levels",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/hub/api-reference"
  },
  {
    "title": "Getting Started",
    "path": "/hub/getting-started",
    "description": "Get up and running with the Nimiq Hub API in minutes",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/hub/getting-started"
  },
  {
    "title": "Account Management",
    "path": "/hub/guide/accounts",
    "description": "Onboard users, manage accounts, and handle addresses",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/hub/guide/accounts"
  },
  {
    "title": "Advanced Features",
    "path": "/hub/guide/advanced",
    "description": "Cashlinks, privileged swap helpers, and multi-chain considerations",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/hub/guide/advanced"
  },
  {
    "title": "Core Concepts",
    "path": "/hub/guide/concepts",
    "description": "Understand the Hub architecture, request behaviors, and security model",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/hub/guide/concepts"
  },
  {
    "title": "Integration Guide",
    "path": "/hub/guide/integration",
    "description": "Best practices for integrating the Nimiq Hub API into your application",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/hub/guide/integration"
  },
  {
    "title": "Transaction Methods",
    "path": "/hub/guide/transactions",
    "description": "Request payments, sign transactions, and interact with the blockchain",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/hub/guide/transactions"
  },
  {
    "title": "Nimiq Hub",
    "path": "/hub",
    "description": "The secure wallet integration API that lets your users sign transactions, manage accounts, and interact with the blockchain — without you ever touching their private keys.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/hub"
  },
  {
    "title": "Legacy Migration Guide (PoW → PoS)",
    "path": "/migration",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/migration"
  },
  {
    "title": "This Page No Longer Exists",
    "path": "/migration/migration-404",
    "description": "The transition from PoW to PoS is complete, and this section has been archived.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/migration/migration-404"
  },
  {
    "title": "Guide for Integrators and Exchanges",
    "path": "/migration/migration-integrators",
    "description": "This guide outlines the key steps and requirements for integrators, such as exchanges, who want to set up and operate a Proof-of-Stake (PoS) node for the Nimiq blockchain. It provides detailed information on node types, hardware requirements and configuration options to facilitate an efficient integration.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/migration/migration-integrators"
  },
  {
    "title": "PoS Migration Guide for JSON-RPC",
    "path": "/migration/migration-json-rpc",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/migration/migration-json-rpc"
  },
  {
    "title": "Migration Technical Details",
    "path": "/migration/migration-technical-details",
    "description": "Nimiq is transitioning from a Proof-of-Work (PoW) to a Proof-of-Stake (PoS) blockchain. This transition is being carried out through a special hard fork, and will happen in three phases: validator registration, pre-staking, and activation.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/migration/migration-technical-details"
  },
  {
    "title": "Migration Guide for Web Developers",
    "path": "/migration/migration-web-developers",
    "description": "With our shift from Proof-of-Work (PoW) to Proof-of-Stake (PoS), the Nimiq Web Client has undergone significant updates. This comparison page is meticulously crafted to guide you through the enhancements implemented in the new version. We'll highlight critical changes in configuration, client instantiation, wallet creation, transaction handling, and more.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/migration/migration-web-developers"
  },
  {
    "title": "Ethereum Provider API (window.ethereum)",
    "path": "/mini-apps/api-reference/ethereum-provider",
    "description": "This provider implements EIP-1193 and supports EIP-6963 provider discovery. It is injected into the mini app environment.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/mini-apps/api-reference/ethereum-provider"
  },
  {
    "title": "API Reference",
    "path": "/mini-apps/api-reference",
    "description": "This API is injected by the host app into the mini app environment. The providers are available on the window object. This section is reference-only.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/mini-apps/api-reference"
  },
  {
    "title": "Nimiq Provider API (window.nimiq)",
    "path": "/mini-apps/api-reference/nimiq-provider",
    "description": "This provider exposes Nimiq blockchain operations and is injected into the mini app environment.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/mini-apps/api-reference/nimiq-provider"
  },
  {
    "title": "Build a Dual-Chain Mini App with Nimiq Pay",
    "path": "/mini-apps/dual-chain-mini-app-tutorial",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/mini-apps/dual-chain-mini-app-tutorial"
  },
  {
    "title": "Load a Local Mini App in Nimiq Pay",
    "path": "/mini-apps/load-local-mini-app",
    "description": "Use this guide to load any locally running web app inside Nimiq Pay.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/mini-apps/load-local-mini-app"
  },
  {
    "title": "Build Your First Nimiq Mini App",
    "path": "/mini-apps/mini-app-tutorial",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/mini-apps/mini-app-tutorial"
  },
  {
    "title": "Mini Apps",
    "path": "/mini-apps/overview",
    "description": "Build mini apps that run inside Nimiq Pay, with optional access to Nimiq and Ethereum wallet features.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/mini-apps/overview"
  },
  {
    "title": "Address Book",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/address-book",
    "description": "Provides mapping of addresses to their corresponding labels for mainnet pools, services, and exchanges.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/address-book"
  },
  {
    "title": "Albatross Policy",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/albatross-policy",
    "description": "Constants and utility functions for blockchain configuration, supply, and block calculations.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/albatross-policy"
  },
  {
    "title": "Browser Detection",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/browser-detection",
    "description": "Utility for detecting browser details including type, version, private mode, and device information.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/browser-detection"
  },
  {
    "title": "Clipboard",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/clipboard",
    "description": "Utility for copying text to the clipboard with mobile compatibility.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/clipboard"
  },
  {
    "title": "Cookie Utilities",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/cookie-utilities",
    "description": "Utility functions for managing cookies including getting, setting, and unsetting.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/cookie-utilities"
  },
  {
    "title": "Currency Info",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/currency-info",
    "description": "Provides currency information including code, symbol, name, and decimal precision.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/currency-info"
  },
  {
    "title": "Fiat API",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/fiat-api",
    "description": "Provides unified interface for accessing fiat exchange rates from various APIs.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/fiat-api"
  },
  {
    "title": "Formattable Number",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/formattable-number",
    "description": "Formats and converts numbers without precision loss with customizable formatting options.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/formattable-number"
  },
  {
    "title": "Nimiq Utils",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils",
    "description": "Production-ready JavaScript utilities for blockchain development. Battle-tested, type-safe, and tree-shakable.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils"
  },
  {
    "title": "Install Nimiq Utils",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/installation",
    "description": "A comprehensive JavaScript/TypeScript utility library for the Nimiq blockchain ecosystem.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/installation"
  },
  {
    "title": "Rate Limit Scheduler",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/rate-limit-scheduler",
    "description": "Controls and limits task execution based on defined rate limits and parallel task limits.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/rate-limit-scheduler"
  },
  {
    "title": "Request Link Encoding",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/request-link-encoding",
    "description": "Creates and parses request links for multiple cryptocurrencies including Nimiq, Bitcoin, and Ethereum.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/request-link-encoding"
  },
  {
    "title": "Staking Rewards Calculator",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/staking-rewards-calculator",
    "description": "Calculates potential wealth accumulation through staking with reward decay, compounding, and fees.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/staking-rewards-calculator"
  },
  {
    "title": "Supply Calculator",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/supply-calculator",
    "description": "Calculates total Proof-of-Stake supply at specific times considering supply decay.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/supply-calculator"
  },
  {
    "title": "Tweenable",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/tweenable",
    "description": "Animation utility for handling tween animations with customizable easing functions.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/tweenable"
  },
  {
    "title": "Utf8 Tools",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/utf8-tools",
    "description": "Utility class for UTF-8 string and byte array conversions, validation, and truncation.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/utf8-tools"
  },
  {
    "title": "Validation Utils",
    "path": "/nimiq-utils/validation-utils",
    "description": "Utility class for validating, normalizing, and checking Nimiq addresses and hashes.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nimiq-utils/validation-utils"
  },
  {
    "title": "Nodes & Validators",
    "path": "/nodes",
    "description": "This hub helps you choose between being a validator, hosting your own RPC endpoint or contributing prover capacity to the zero-knowledge sync.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nodes"
  },
  {
    "title": "Prover node",
    "path": "/nodes/prover-node-guide",
    "description": "This guide will help you configure your node as a prover node. Follow the steps below to set up your prover node.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nodes/prover-node-guide"
  },
  {
    "title": "Becoming a Validator",
    "path": "/nodes/validators/becoming-a-validator",
    "description": "This guide will walk you through the process of setting up a Nimiq validator in the Albatross network.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nodes/validators/becoming-a-validator"
  },
  {
    "title": "Comprehensive Staking FAQ",
    "path": "/nodes/validators/staking-faq",
    "description": "Staking allows you to delegate your NIM to a validator, helping secure the network while earning rewards. Whether you're new to staking or looking for details about validators, rewards, or the Proof-of-Stake protocol, this FAQ covers everything you need to know. Explore topics such as unstaking periods, selecting a staking pool, and contributing to decentralization.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nodes/validators/staking-faq"
  },
  {
    "title": "Staking Pools Handbook",
    "path": "/nodes/validators/staking-handbook",
    "description": "This handbook provides guidelines for the staking pools on the Nimiq network. It covers best practices, a code of conduct, and general standards required to ensure a fair and transparent experience for the stakers. The document outlines the requirements for staking pools, which are based on single validators, to integrate with the Nimiq Wallet. This integration helps stakers make informed decisions when choosing where to stake their funds.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nodes/validators/staking-handbook"
  },
  {
    "title": "Nimiq Validator Trustscore",
    "path": "/nodes/validators/validator-trustscore",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/nodes/validators/validator-trustscore"
  },
  {
    "title": "Accounts",
    "path": "/protocol/accounts",
    "description": "Nimiq has four account types, each with unique features and purposes. Each account has an individual address assigned, enabling users to interact with the blockchain.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/accounts"
  },
  {
    "title": "Block Format",
    "path": "/protocol/consensus/block-format",
    "description": "In the Nimiq blockchain, blocks are categorized into microblocks and macroblocks. Each type has a different role in maintaining the blockchain:",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/consensus/block-format"
  },
  {
    "title": "Equivocation Proofs",
    "path": "/protocol/consensus/equivocation-proofs",
    "description": "An equivocation refers to malicious behavior by a validator that contradicts the consensus, such as producing two blocks at the same block height or signing two different proposals within the same Tendermint aggregation.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/consensus/equivocation-proofs"
  },
  {
    "title": "Punishments",
    "path": "/protocol/consensus/punishments",
    "description": "Validators are responsible for ensuring the security and stability of a blockchain network. They are rewarded for participating and punished if they fail to contribute according to the consensus. Misbehaving always results in either losing the rewards or going to jail, where the validator gets locked up for a period and loses the rewards. The types of punishment are divided based on the severity of the misbehavior.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/consensus/punishments"
  },
  {
    "title": "Verifiable Random Functions",
    "path": "/protocol/consensus/verifiable-random-functions",
    "description": "A verifiable random function is a pseudo-random function that generates a random value and allows anyone to verify if it was correctly calculated. It combines a proving function, a verifying function, and an extracting function. A key pair is used to operate these functions.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/consensus/verifiable-random-functions"
  },
  {
    "title": "Rewards",
    "path": "/protocol/economics/rewards",
    "description": "Validators stake a minimum deposit to participate in block production. Following consensus rules yields rewards; failing to do so results in punishments and loss of rewards.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/economics/rewards"
  },
  {
    "title": "Glossary Nimiq PoS",
    "path": "/protocol/glossary",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/glossary"
  },
  {
    "title": "Nimiq Proof-of-Stake",
    "path": "/protocol",
    "description": "Welcome to the Nimiq Proof-of-Stake documentation! This section introduces our consensus protocol, Albatross, and provides a comprehensive overview of our protocol's unique features.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol"
  },
  {
    "title": "Architecture",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync/architecture",
    "description": "Nimiq's consensus architecture centers on a two-phase synchronization model that separates epoch-level state synchronization from real-time block processing. This design enables efficient sync strategies tailored to different node capabilities while maintaining cryptographic security guarantees.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync/architecture"
  },
  {
    "title": "Sync and Network Consensus",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync",
    "description": "Design-level overview of how Nimiq nodes synchronize with the blockchain, across all node types and sync modes.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync"
  },
  {
    "title": "Block Live Sync",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync/live-sync/block-live-sync",
    "description": "Block Live Sync is the real-time block synchronization mechanism, used as the second phase of synchronization after macro sync completes. It enables nodes to follow the chain tip by processing micro blocks as they are produced. This mode targets nodes that do not require state synchronization.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync/live-sync/block-live-sync"
  },
  {
    "title": "State Live Sync",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync/live-sync/state-live-sync",
    "description": "State Live Sync is a comprehensive real-time synchronization mechanism designed exclusively for full blockchain instances. The State Live Sync simultaneously maintains both the latest blockchain state and complete account data through parallel block and state synchronization.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync/live-sync/state-live-sync"
  },
  {
    "title": "History Macro Sync",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync/macro-sync/history-macro-sync",
    "description": "History Macro Sync is a full history synchronization mechanism designed exclusively for history nodes. It downloads the complete blockchain transaction history from genesis. The History Macro Sync is required for nodes that need to serve historical queries or verify the full transaction history.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync/macro-sync/history-macro-sync"
  },
  {
    "title": "Light Macro Sync",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync/macro-sync/light-macro-sync",
    "description": "Light Macro Sync is a robust, ZKP-based synchronization mechanism designed for secure blockchain state verification. It operates with both full and light blockchain instances and provides cryptographically verified synchronization by leveraging Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) to verify the macro chain validity before application to the local blockchain.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync/macro-sync/light-macro-sync"
  },
  {
    "title": "Pico Macro Sync",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync/macro-sync/pico-macro-sync",
    "description": "Pico Macro Sync is a lightweight sync mechanism designed for resource-constrained environments. It operates exclusively with light blockchain instances and provides fast initial synchronization by requesting only essential macro blocks (election and checkpoint blocks) from peers on a per-peer basis. Pico Macro Sync takes an optimistic approach by accepting macro blocks with minimal validation, then falling back to a more robust sync method if conflicts are detected.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync/macro-sync/pico-macro-sync"
  },
  {
    "title": "Network Messages",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync/network-protocol",
    "description": "The consensus crate implements a comprehensive network protocol enabling nodes to synchronize with the blockchain and exchange data efficiently. Communication follows two primary patterns:",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync/network-protocol"
  },
  {
    "title": "Traits and Abstractions",
    "path": "/protocol/node-sync/traits-and-abstractions",
    "description": "Three core traits form the foundation of the consensus system, creating a pluggable synchronization architecture:",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/node-sync/traits-and-abstractions"
  },
  {
    "title": "History Store",
    "path": "/protocol/storage/history-store",
    "description": "The History Store is a component of the blockchain that is responsible for recording and maintaining all transactions that have occurred on the blockchain. This system ensures the integrity and traceability of transactions, serving as both a record-keeping and indexing tool. It allows easy retrieval of transactions, such as locating all transactions associated with a specific address.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/storage/history-store"
  },
  {
    "title": "Mempool",
    "path": "/protocol/storage/mempool",
    "description": "",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/storage/mempool"
  },
  {
    "title": "Merkle Trees",
    "path": "/protocol/storage/merkle-trees",
    "description": "In the context of blockchain, ensuring data security is paramount. Our blockchain uses a tree-like structure known as Merkle Trees to store accounts and transactions. This approach guarantees that every piece of data is encoded within these trees, ultimately resulting in a single value called the \"root.\" This root value represents the entire dataset and acts as a commitment to the data being stored in the blocks.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/storage/merkle-trees"
  },
  {
    "title": "Transactions",
    "path": "/protocol/transactions",
    "description": "Transactions modify the state of the Nimiq blockchain according to protocol rules and enable value transfers and contract interactions. These transactions are stored in the micro block's body. This document provides a technical overview of transaction structure, common transaction issues, transaction finality, and system operations known as inherents in the Nimiq blockchain.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/transactions"
  },
  {
    "title": "Skip Blocks",
    "path": "/protocol/validators/skip-blocks",
    "description": "Skip blocks are a special type of micro block in the consensus protocol. When a validator fails to produce a block within the expected timeframe, the remaining elected validators can collectively agree to add a skip block instead of the missing micro block. This prevents delays and ensures the blockchain progresses smoothly. Once a supermajority of elected validators agrees to add the skip block, the chain resumes its regular progression.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/validators/skip-blocks"
  },
  {
    "title": "Slots",
    "path": "/protocol/validators/slots",
    "description": "Validators are selected from the validator set to integrate the following validator list based on their initial stake. Validators that have deposited a higher stake increase their chances of being selected for the upcoming epoch. At the end of an epoch, the block leader proposes a new validator list in the election macro block, including the group of validators eligible to participate in the consensus.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/validators/slots"
  },
  {
    "title": "Stakers",
    "path": "/protocol/validators/stakers",
    "description": "A staker delegates its NIM to a validator, which validates blocks on its behalf. Validators handle the reward distribution off-chain. This document explains how the staking process works, the functions available to stakers, and the conditions and rules that govern these states.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/validators/stakers"
  },
  {
    "title": "Staking Contract",
    "path": "/protocol/validators/staking-contract",
    "description": "The staking contract is a special contract serving as a central repository for the data related to validators, stakers, and the staking process. It is initially hardcoded at the genesis block, and thereon, any modification in the balances or state of validators and stakers is updated at every block by validators. Any node on Nimiq's blockchain with a wallet and stake can propose to become a validator or a staker.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/validators/staking-contract"
  },
  {
    "title": "Validator Keys",
    "path": "/protocol/validators/validator-keys",
    "description": "Validators hold a set of keys in order to participate in the consensus. Besides a validator address, which serves as the validator’s identifier, validators hold three key pairs: a cold, a warm, and a hot key pair. These keys enable validators to sign several transactions.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/validators/validator-keys"
  },
  {
    "title": "Validators",
    "path": "/protocol/validators/validators",
    "description": "Validators are the block producers of PoS blockchains. They are responsible for processing and validating transactions, block validation, and maintaining the integrity of the network. Their primary function is to preserve the network's consensus by agreeing on the current state. By maintaining consensus and actively participating in the network, validators earn rewards in the form of transaction fees and block rewards.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/validators/validators"
  },
  {
    "title": "ZKP and Recursive SNARKs",
    "path": "/protocol/zkp/zkp-and-recursive-snarks",
    "description": "Nimiq supports several types of users and syncing mechanisms. Light and full nodes can synchronize to the network by requesting a proof of the current state of the blockchain. The blockchain implements zero-knowledge proofs capable of proving most of the chain in a small proof.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/zkp/zkp-and-recursive-snarks"
  },
  {
    "title": "Prover Node",
    "path": "/protocol/zkp/prover-node",
    "description": "A prover node is a particular type of node that generates zero-knowledge proofs (zkp). A zero-knowledge proof is a method of proving a statement between two parties - a prover and a verifier. Read this post for a detailed description of zero-knowledge proofs.",
    "locale": "docs",
    "url": "http://localhost:3000/protocol/zkp/prover-node"
  },
  {
